title: Interpret Operator Stats On The VISU_DATA Screen
summary: Use the Operator Stats section on the VISU_DATA screen to identify and interpret Resp Time Max, Resp Time Min, and Resp Time Avg values in milliseconds for the left and right tippers.
when_to_use: Use this reference procedure when viewing the VISU_DATA screen to read operator response-time statistics for the left and right tippers and understand what each displayed metric represents.
not_for: Do not use this source to determine acceptable response-time thresholds.
Do not use this procedure if the Operator Stats fields are missing or unreadable.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Open the VISU_DATA screen, locate the Operator Stats section, and read the displayed response-time metrics for the left and right tippers.

safety_notes: This source describes interpretation of displayed HMI statistics only.
Do not infer acceptable response-time thresholds from this source section.
Escalate if the Operator Stats fields are missing or unreadable.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to the operator station HMI

access_required: Access to the operator station HMI
system_preconditions: The VISU_DATA screen is available for viewing.
The Operator Stats section is visible and readable on the HMI.

external_references.quote_or_summary: The Operator Stats section provides Resp Time Max (ms), Resp Time Min (ms), and Resp Time Avg (ms) for left and right tippers, measuring the time it took an operator to press the button to initiate tipping after the tote was gripped.

visual_references.description: Operator Station HMI data screen showing the Operator Stats section with Resp Time Max, Resp Time Min, and Resp Time Avg for left and right tippers.
Operator Station HMI VISU_DATA screen layout for locating the data screen and nearby statistics areas.

steps.title: Open the VISU_DATA screen and locate Operator Stats
steps.purpose: Find the HMI area that contains the operator response-time statistics.
steps.instruction: Open the VISU_DATA screen on the operator station HMI and locate the Operator Stats section.
steps.expected_result: The Operator Stats section is visible on the VISU_DATA screen.
steps.healthy_condition: The Operator Stats section can be clearly seen and the response-time fields are readable.
steps.failure_condition: The VISU_DATA screen cannot be opened, or the Operator Stats section is missing or unreadable.

actions.label: Open VISU_DATA screen
actions.exact_action: Open the VISU_DATA screen on the operator station HMI.

actions.label: Locate Operator Stats
actions.exact_action: Locate the Operator Stats section on the VISU_DATA screen.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The Operator Stats section and the Resp Time Max, Resp Time Min, and Resp Time Avg fields for left and right tippers.
Overall VISU_DATA screen layout to help identify the correct operator station data screen.

stop_or_escalate_if: The Operator Stats fields are missing.
The Operator Stats fields are unreadable.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The Operator Stats section provides Resp Time Max (ms), Resp Time Min (ms), and Resp Time Avg (ms) for left and right tippers.

steps.title: Read Resp Time Max values
steps.purpose: Identify the longest operator response time shown for each tipper.
steps.instruction: Read the Resp Time Max (ms) fields and identify the longest time it took an operator to press the button to initiate tipping after the tote was gripped for the left and right tippers.
steps.expected_result: The maximum response-time values in milliseconds are identified for both the left and right tippers.
steps.healthy_condition: Both left and right Resp Time Max values are visible and can be read directly from the HMI.
steps.failure_condition: One or both Resp Time Max values are not visible, not readable, or cannot be interpreted from the displayed fields.

actions.label: Read Resp Time Max
actions.exact_action: Read the Resp Time Max (ms) fields for the left and right tippers.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The Resp Time Max (ms) fields and the left/right field positions in the Operator Stats section.

stop_or_escalate_if: The Resp Time Max fields are missing or unreadable.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Resp Time Max (ms) ... For each metric, the left field corresponds to the left tipper and the right field corresponds to the right tipper, measuring the time it took an operator to press the button to initiate tipping after the tote was gripped.

steps.title: Read Resp Time Min values
steps.purpose: Identify the shortest operator response time shown for each tipper.
steps.instruction: Read the Resp Time Min (ms) fields and identify the shortest time it took an operator to press the button to initiate tipping after the tote was gripped for the left and right tippers.
steps.expected_result: The minimum response-time values in milliseconds are identified for both the left and right tippers.
steps.healthy_condition: Both left and right Resp Time Min values are visible and can be read directly from the HMI.
steps.failure_condition: One or both Resp Time Min values are not visible, not readable, or cannot be interpreted from the displayed fields.

actions.label: Read Resp Time Min
actions.exact_action: Read the Resp Time Min (ms) fields for the left and right tippers.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Resp Time Min (ms) ... For each metric, the left field corresponds to the left tipper and the right field corresponds to the right tipper, measuring the time it took an operator to press the button to initiate tipping after the tote was gripped.

steps.title: Read Resp Time Avg values
steps.purpose: Identify the average operator response time shown for each tipper.
steps.instruction: Read the Resp Time Avg (ms) fields and identify the average time it took an operator to press the button to initiate tipping after the tote was gripped for the left and right tippers.
steps.expected_result: The average response-time values in milliseconds are identified for both the left and right tippers.
steps.healthy_condition: Both left and right Resp Time Avg values are visible and can be read directly from the HMI.
steps.failure_condition: One or both Resp Time Avg values are not visible, not readable, or cannot be interpreted from the displayed fields.

actions.label: Read Resp Time Avg
actions.exact_action: Read the Resp Time Avg (ms) fields for the left and right tippers.

steps.title: Compare and record the displayed metrics
steps.purpose: Capture the displayed left and right response-time statistics for reference or comparison.
steps.instruction: Compare the left and right values if needed and record the displayed response-time metrics in milliseconds.
steps.expected_result: The displayed left and right Resp Time Max, Resp Time Min, and Resp Time Avg values are documented as shown on the HMI.
steps.healthy_condition: The values are recorded exactly as displayed and any left/right comparison is based only on the shown metrics.
steps.failure_condition: The values cannot be reliably recorded because the fields are missing, unreadable, or ambiguous.

actions.label: Compare left and right values
actions.exact_action: Compare the left and right displayed response-time values if needed.

actions.label: Record displayed metrics
actions.exact_action: Record the displayed response-time metrics in milliseconds.

success_criteria: The Operator Stats section is located on the VISU_DATA screen.
Resp Time Max, Resp Time Min, and Resp Time Avg values are identified for both left and right tippers.
The user correctly interprets these values as operator response times measured from tote grip to button press initiating tipping.
The displayed metrics are recorded in milliseconds if needed.
healthy_conditions: The Operator Stats section is visible and readable.
Left and right fields for Resp Time Max, Resp Time Min, and Resp Time Avg are present.
Values are interpreted only as documented by the source.
failure_conditions: The Operator Stats section is missing.
The Operator Stats fields are unreadable.
The displayed values cannot be clearly associated with left and right tippers.
A user attempts to derive acceptable thresholds from this source section.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if the Operator Stats fields are missing or unreadable.
Escalate if the VISU_DATA screen cannot be used to clearly identify the left and right response-time metrics.
Do not infer acceptable response-time thresholds from this source; seek additional guidance if thresholds are needed.
